title: Tomb of Amenemhat (Beni Hasan)
description: Tomb BH2 in Beni Hasan necropolis, dating to the 12th Dynasty. Famous for its wrestling scenes. (Area: Beni Hasan, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Khnumhotep II (Beni Hasan)
description: Tomb BH3, a large rock-cut tomb of a profound governor. Famous for the depiction of the "Asiatic Caravan". (Area: Beni Hasan, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Baqet III (Beni Hasan)
description: Tomb BH15, known for murals depicting wrestling techniques, gazelles, and mythical creatures. (Area: Beni Hasan, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Khety (Beni Hasan)
description: Tomb BH17, features scenes of daily life, games, and military training. (Area: Beni Hasan, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Speos Artemidos (Istabl Antar)
description: Rock-cut temple dedicated to the goddess Pakhet, built by Queen Hatshepsut. (Area: Beni Hasan, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Great Temple of the Aten (Tell el-Amarna)
description: The principal temple of Aten in Akhetaten, a vast open-air structure. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Small Temple of the Aten (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Hwt-Aten, located near the Royal House, likely for the King's private worship. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Northern Palace (Tell el-Amarna)
description: A residence for the royal family, possibly Queen Nefertiti or Meritaten, known for nature paintings. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Meryre I (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna North Tomb 4, belonging to the High Priest of the Aten. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Huya (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna North Tomb 1, steward to Queen Tiye. Contains scenes of a royal banquet. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Ahmose (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna North Tomb 3, belonging to a royal fan-bearer. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Panehesy (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna North Tomb 6, later used as a Coptic church. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom/Coptic)

title: Royal Tomb of Akhenaten (Tell el-Amarna)
description: The burial place of Akhenaten and Meketaten in a remote wadi. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Boundary Stela U (Tell el-Amarna)
description: One of the large stelae carved into the cliffs defining the limits of Akhetaten. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Ay (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna South Tomb 25, famous for the full version of the Great Hymn to the Aten. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Tomb of Petosiris (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: A temple-like tomb combining Egyptian and Greek artistic styles. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: Ptolemaic)

title: Tomb of Isadora (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: Known as the tomb of the "martyr of love", a wealthy young woman who drowned in the Nile. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: Roman)

title: Catacombs of the Ibis and Baboon (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: Vast underground galleries containing thousands of mummified sacred animals of Thoth. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: Late Period/Ptolemaic)

title: Boundary Stela A (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: One of the western boundary stelae of Akhenaten's city, carved into the cliff face. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Hermopolis Magna Basilica (El Ashmunein)
description: Remains of a Coptic Basilica built over a Ptolemaic temple, with towering granite columns. (Area: Ashmunein, Era: Coptic/Ptolemaic)

title: Temple of Thoth (El Ashmunein)
description: Sparse remains of the great temple dedicated to the god of wisdom. (Area: Ashmunein, Era: New Kingdom/Roman)

title: Zawyet el-Maiyitin Pyramid
description: A small step pyramid from the late 3rd Dynasty, the only pyramid on the east bank of the Nile. (Area: Zawyet el-Maiyitin, Era: Old Kingdom)

title: The City of the Dead (Zawyet el-Maiyitin)
description: One of the largest cemeteries in the world, with countless white domes stretching to the horizon. (Area: Zawyet el-Maiyitin, Era: Islamic/Modern)

title: Monastery of the Virgin Mary (Gabal al-Tayr)
description: An ancient monastery atop a cliff, housing a cave believed to be visited by the Holy Family. (Area: Samalut, Era: Coptic)

title: Kom al-Kuffar (Gabal al-Tayr)
description: Ancient quarries and tombs located near the monastery area. (Area: Samalut, Era: Pharaonic/Coptic)

title: Tombs of Fraser (Tihna el-Gebel)
description: Rock-cut tombs of the Old Kingdom (5th Dynasty), rarely visited but well-preserved. (Area: Tihna, Era: Old Kingdom)

title: Temple of Akoris (Tihna el-Gebel)
description: Ruins of a temple dedicated to Sobek and Amun, built partly into the rock. (Area: Tihna, Era: Ptolemaic/Roman)

title: Antinopolis Ruins (Sheikh Ibada)
description: City founded by Emperor Hadrian in memory of Antinous. Includes remains of a hippodrome and temples. (Area: Mallawi East, Era: Roman)

title: Tomb of Djehutihotep (Deir el-Bersha)
description: Famous for the "colossal statue transport" scene, though heavily damaged. (Area: Deir el-Bersha, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Deir el-Bersha Necropolis
description: Chief burial place for the Nomarchs of the 15th nome. (Area: Deir el-Bersha, Era: Middle Kingdom)

title: Oxyrhynchus (El-Bahnasa)
description: Site of the ancient city of the Sharp-nosed Fish, famous for thousands of papyrus finds. (Area: Beni Mazar, Era: Greco-Roman)

title: Tombs of the Companions (El-Bahnasa)
description: Revered Islamic shrine containing tombs of companions of the Prophet Muhammad from the conquest of Egypt. (Area: Beni Mazar, Era: Islamic)

title: Tree of Mary (El-Bahnasa)
description: An ancient tree where local tradition says the Holy Family rested. (Area: Beni Mazar, Era: Coptic Tradition)

title: Al-Lamati Mosque
description: A historic Fatimid-era mosque located in the heart of Minya city. (Area: Minya City, Era: Islamic/Fatimid)

title: Al-Amrawi Mosque
description: One of the oldest mosques in the city, recently restored. (Area: Minya City, Era: Islamic)

title: Mallawi Museum
description: Specifically houses artifacts recovered from sites in Minya like Tuna el-Gebel and Hermopolis. (Area: Mallawi, Era: Modern/Various)

title: Palace of Abdel-Magid Basha (Mallawi)
description: A historic architectural landmark reflecting early 20th-century grandeur. (Area: Mallawi, Era: Modern)

title: Kom el-Ahmar (Sawaris)
description: Ancient Hebnu, capital of the 16th nome, featuring Old Kingdom tombs. (Area: Near Minya, Era: Old Kingdom)

title: Tombs of El-Sheikh Said
description: Rock-cut tombs of the nomarchs of the Hare Nome (15th nome). (Area: Opposite Mallawi, Era: Old Kingdom)

title: Catacombs of the Sacred Bulls (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: Large underground galleries dedicated to the burial of sacred bulls. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: Late Period)

title: Roman Waterwheel (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: A deep Roman well with a spiral staircase, providing water to the sacred animal necropolis. (Area: Tuna el-Gebel, Era: Roman)

title: Church of the Holy Virgin (Deir al-Adhra)
description: Rock-hewn church built by Empress Helena, part of the Gabal al-Tayr complex. (Area: Samalut, Era: Coptic/Byzantine)

title: Tomb of Mahu (Tell el-Amarna)
description: Amarna South Tomb 9, belonging to the Chief of Police of Akhetaten. (Area: Amarna, Era: New Kingdom)

title: Mar Girgis Church (Biba - border)
description: Ancient Coptic church (often associated with Beni Suef but culturally linked to Minya diocese tours). (Area: North Minya border, Era: Coptic)

title: Al-Saba Banat Tombs (El-Bahnasa)
description: Tombs of the "Seven Girls" who participated in the battle against Romans. (Area: Beni Mazar, Era: Islamic)

title: Deir Abu Hennis
description: Contains ancient quarries converted into a church with early Coptic paintings. (Area: East Bank Mallawi, Era: Coptic)

title: Temple of Rameses II (Sheikh Ibada)
description: Remains of a temple built by Rameses II, later incorporated into Antinopolis. (Area: Sheikh Ibada, Era: New Kingdom)
